den_contract_contract.description: A régi fieldet csak későbbi breaking release-ben vagy igazolt migration után távolítja el.

followUps.den_contract_evolution_followup: Hogyan mérnéd, hogy a kliensek még függnek-e egy response fieldtől?

questions.title: Designing a universal data exchange layer
questions.prompt: Several business units and external customers use different representations of the same intellectual-property concepts. How would you design the translation layer?
questions.answerStructureHint: Contracts, translation boundaries, ownership, and failure handling.

expectedPoints.label: Defines explicit external contracts
expectedPoints.description: Uses versioned schemas, validation, ownership, and clear error semantics for each integration surface.

expectedPoints.label: Protects bounded contexts
expectedPoints.description: Maps at boundaries and avoids making one canonical transport model the internal domain model of every service.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves traceability
expectedPoints.description: Keeps source identifiers, correlation data, mapping version, and processing history for support and replay.

expectedPoints.label: Handles partial or unknown data
expectedPoints.description: Defines reject, quarantine, default, and manual-resolution policies with monitoring.

followUps.prompt: When is a canonical data model useful, and when does it become harmful?

hu.title: Universal data exchange layer tervezése
hu.prompt: Több business unit és external customer ugyanazokat az intellectual-property fogalmakat eltérően reprezentálja. Hogyan terveznéd meg a translation layert?
hu.answerStructureHint: Contractok, translation boundaryk, ownership és failure handling.

den_exchange_contracts.label: Explicit external contractokat definiál
den_exchange_contracts.description: Versioned schemát, validationt, ownershipet és tiszta error semanticset használ minden integration surface-höz.

den_exchange_boundaries.label: Védi a bounded contexteket
den_exchange_boundaries.description: Boundaryn mapel, és nem teszi az egy canonical transport modellt minden service belső domain modelljévé.

den_exchange_trace.label: Megőrzi a traceabilityt
den_exchange_trace.description: Source identifiert, correlation datát, mapping versiont és processing historyt tart supporthoz és replayhez.

den_exchange_failure.label: Kezeli a részleges vagy ismeretlen adatot
den_exchange_failure.description: Reject-, quarantine-, default- és manual-resolution policyt definiál monitoringgal.

followUps.den_data_exchange_translation_followup: Mikor hasznos a canonical data model, és mikor válik károssá?

questions.title: Securing an API and data exchange platform
questions.prompt: What security controls would you expect in a customer-facing API and data exchange platform beyond simply requiring login?
questions.answerStructureHint: Identity, authorization, data protection, abuse resistance, and operations.

expectedPoints.label: Separates authentication and authorization
expectedPoints.description: Uses scoped permissions, least privilege, tenant isolation, and object-level checks rather than trusting identity alone.

expectedPoints.label: Protects inputs and sensitive data
expectedPoints.description: Covers schema validation, injection prevention, transport encryption, secret management, and sensitive-data minimization.

expectedPoints.label: Limits abuse
expectedPoints.description: Mentions rate limits, quotas, payload limits, timeouts, replay protection, or idempotency for sensitive operations.

expectedPoints.label: Designs for detection and response
expectedPoints.description: Includes audit logs, security telemetry, dependency scanning, key rotation, and incident procedures.

followUps.prompt: How would you prevent a valid customer token from reading another customer's records?

hu.title: API- és data exchange platform védelme
hu.prompt: Milyen security controlokat várnál el egy customer-facing API- és data exchange platformon a puszta login-követelményen túl?
hu.answerStructureHint: Identity, authorization, data protection, abuse resistance és operations.

den_security_authz.label: Elválasztja az authenticationt és authorizationt
den_security_authz.description: Scoped permissiont, least privilege-et, tenant isolationt és object-level checket használ, nem bízik pusztán az identityben.

den_security_input_data.label: Védi az inputot és sensitive datát
den_security_input_data.description: Lefedi a schema validationt, injection preventiont, transport encryptiont, secret managementet és data minimizationt.

den_security_abuse.label: Korlátozza az abuse-t
den_security_abuse.description: Rate limitet, quotát, payload limitet, timeoutot, replay protectiont vagy idempotencyt említ.

den_security_ops.label: Detectionre és response-ra tervez
den_security_ops.description: Audit logot, security telemetryt, dependency scanninget, key rotationt és incident procedure-t foglal bele.

followUps.den_api_security_followup: Hogyan akadályoznád meg, hogy egy valid customer token egy másik customer rekordjait olvassa?

questions.title: When CQRS is not justified
questions.prompt: A team proposes separate read and write databases for a simple CRUD service. How would you decide whether CQRS is justified?
questions.answerStructureHint: Measured problem, smallest useful separation, and complexity cost.

expectedPoints.label: Looks for a real asymmetry
expectedPoints.description: Asks whether reads and writes differ materially in rules, shape, volume, latency, scale, or ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses the smallest useful form
expectedPoints.description: Considers separate handlers or models in one application before separate databases and infrastructure.

expectedPoints.label: Evaluates consistency and operations
expectedPoints.description: Accounts for event delivery, projection rebuilds, monitoring, data repair, and eventual consistency.

expectedPoints.label: Can reject the pattern
expectedPoints.description: Recommends ordinary CRUD or a modular design when the added complexity has no evidence-backed benefit.

followUps.prompt: What is the smallest CQRS-like separation you could introduce without asynchronous projections?

hu.title: Mikor nem indokolt a CQRS?
hu.prompt: Egy csapat külön read és write adatbázist javasol egy egyszerű CRUD service-hez. Hogyan döntenéd el, indokolt-e a CQRS?
hu.answerStructureHint: Mért probléma, legkisebb hasznos szeparáció és complexity cost.

den_cqrs_need.label: Valós aszimmetriát keres
den_cqrs_need.description: Megvizsgálja, hogy a read és write valóban eltér-e rule, shape, volume, latency, scale vagy ownership szempontból.

den_cqrs_incremental.label: A legkisebb hasznos formát választja
den_cqrs_incremental.description: Külön handler vagy model használatát mérlegeli egy applicationben, mielőtt külön database-t és infrastruktúrát vezetne be.

den_cqrs_costs.label: Értékeli a consistency- és operational költséget
den_cqrs_costs.description: Számol event deliveryvel, projection rebuilddel, monitoringgal, data repairrel és eventual consistencyvel.

den_cqrs_reject.label: Képes elutasítani a patternt
den_cqrs_reject.description: Ordinary CRUD-ot vagy modular designt ajánl, ha nincs evidence-backed előny.

questions.title: Designing for eventual consistency
questions.prompt: A command returns success, but the query API may show the old state for several seconds. How would you make this behavior correct and understandable?
questions.answerStructureHint: Consistency boundary, client contract, UX, and repair.

expectedPoints.label: Defines the authoritative write boundary
expectedPoints.description: Keeps immediate invariants and duplicate prevention in the service or transaction that owns the write.

expectedPoints.label: Makes staleness explicit
expectedPoints.description: Defines expected delay and response semantics instead of presenting an asynchronously updated read as immediately current.

expectedPoints.label: Designs a usable client experience
expectedPoints.description: Uses operation status, optimistic UI with confirmation, polling, push updates, or direct command result where appropriate.

expectedPoints.label: Provides observability and repair
expectedPoints.description: Tracks lag, failed projections, retries, dead letters, and reconciliation.

followUps.prompt: How would you provide read-your-writes behavior without making every read strongly consistent?

hu.title: Eventual consistency kezelése

hu.answerStructureHint: Consistency boundary, client contract, UX és repair.

den_eventual_authority.label: Definiálja az authoritative write boundaryt
den_eventual_authority.description: Az immediate invariantokat és duplicate preventiont a write-ot birtokló service-ben vagy transactionben tartja.

den_eventual_contract.label: Explicitté teszi a stalenesst
den_eventual_contract.description: Elvárt késést és response semanticset definiál, nem állítja az asynchronous readet azonnal aktuálisnak.

den_eventual_ux.label: Használható client experience-et tervez
den_eventual_ux.description: Operation statust, confirmationnel működő optimistic UI-t, pollingot, push update-et vagy direct command resultot használ.

den_eventual_repair.label: Observabilityt és repairt biztosít
den_eventual_repair.description: Projection laget, failed projectiont, retryt, dead lettert és reconciliationt követ.

followUps.den_eventual_consistency_followup: Hogyan biztosítanál read-your-writes viselkedést anélkül, hogy minden read strongly consistent lenne?

questions.title: Finding bounded contexts
questions.prompt: How would you identify bounded contexts for an intellectual-property platform used by filing, payments, renewals, and reporting teams?
questions.answerStructureHint: Language, rules, ownership, interactions, and validation.

expectedPoints.label: Uses domain language and rules

expectedPoints.label: Makes ownership explicit
expectedPoints.description: Identifies which context owns each decision and data change rather than drawing only technical layers.

expectedPoints.label: Maps context relationships
expectedPoints.description: Describes upstream and downstream relationships, integration contracts, and translation boundaries.

expectedPoints.label: Validates boundaries empirically
expectedPoints.description: Checks change coupling, coordination cost, transaction needs, scale, and team cognitive load.

hu.title: Bounded contexte-k azonosítása

hu.answerStructureHint: Language, rule-ok, ownership, interactionök és validáció.

den_context_language.label: Domain language-et és rule-okat használ
den_context_language.description: Eltérő jelentésű termeket, valamint együtt változó invariant- és workflow-csoportokat keres.

den_context_ownership.label: Explicitté teszi az ownershipet
den_context_ownership.description: Azonosítja, melyik context birtokolja az egyes döntéseket és data change-eket, nem csak technical layereket rajzol.

den_context_relationships.label: Feltérképezi a contextkapcsolatokat
den_context_relationships.description: Leírja az upstream/downstream kapcsolatokat, integration contractokat és translation boundaryket.

den_context_validate.label: Empirikusan validálja a boundaryket
den_context_validate.description: Change coupling, coordination cost, transaction need, scale és team cognitive load alapján ellenőriz.

followUps.den_bounded_context_followup: Mondj példát, amikor a customer szó két contextben mást jelenthet.

questions.title: Entities, value objects, and aggregates
questions.prompt: Explain entities, value objects, aggregates, and aggregate roots. How do you decide an aggregate boundary?
questions.answerStructureHint: Definitions, invariants, transaction boundary, and size trade-off.

expectedPoints.label: Distinguishes entities and value objects
expectedPoints.description: Uses identity for entities and value-based immutable semantics for value objects.

expectedPoints.label: Defines aggregate and root
expectedPoints.description: Explains that the aggregate is a consistency boundary and external changes go through its root.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses boundaries by invariants
expectedPoints.description: Groups only data and rules that must be consistent together immediately.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids oversized aggregates
expectedPoints.description: Recognizes that large aggregates increase contention, load cost, and coupling and may communicate through events.

followUps.prompt: Should another aggregate store a direct object reference to the first aggregate, or only its identity? Why?

hu.title: Entity, value object és aggregate
hu.prompt: Magyarázd el az entity, value object, aggregate és aggregate root fogalmát. Hogyan választasz aggregate boundaryt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Definíciók, invariantok, transaction boundary és size trade-off.

den_ddd_entity_value.label: Megkülönbözteti az entityt és value objectet
den_ddd_entity_value.description: Az entityt identity, a value objectet value-based immutable semantics alapján írja le.

den_ddd_aggregate.label: Definiálja az aggregate-et és rootot
den_ddd_aggregate.description: Elmagyarázza, hogy az aggregate consistency boundary, és az external change-ek a rooton keresztül mennek.

den_ddd_boundary.label: Invariant alapján választ boundaryt
den_ddd_boundary.description: Csak azokat az adatokat és rule-okat fogja össze, amelyeknek azonnal együtt kell konzisztensnek lenniük.

den_ddd_tradeoff.label: Kerüli a túl nagy aggregate-et
den_ddd_tradeoff.description: Felismeri, hogy a nagy aggregate contentiont, load costot és couplingot okoz, és eventtel kommunikálhat.

followUps.den_ddd_tactical_followup: Egy másik aggregate közvetlen object reference-et tároljon az első aggregate-re, vagy csak az identityjét? Miért?

questions.title: Choosing microservice boundaries

expectedPoints.label: Starts from business capabilities

expectedPoints.label: Assigns clear data ownership

expectedPoints.label: Tests independent change
expectedPoints.description: Looks for independent deployment, failure isolation, scaling, security, and team ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Accounts for distributed-system cost
expectedPoints.description: Includes network failures, observability, versioned contracts, consistency, and operational maturity; considers a modular monolith.

hu.title: Microservice boundaryk megválasztása

hu.answerStructureHint: Business capability, data ownership, independence és operational cost.

den_ms_capability.label: Business capabilityből indul
den_ms_capability.description: Koherens rule-okat és workflow-kat csoportosít, nem table, controller vagy technical layer szerint vág.

den_ms_data.label: Tiszta data ownershipet rendel
den_ms_data.description: Egy service-nek ad write authorityt, és megakadályozza a cross-service direct database accesst.

den_ms_independence.label: Independent change-et vizsgál
den_ms_independence.description: Independent deploymentet, failure isolationt, scalinget, securityt és team ownershipet keres.

den_ms_cost.label: Számol a distributed-system költséggel
den_ms_cost.description: Network failure-t, observabilityt, versioned contractot, consistencyt és operational maturityt vesz figyelembe; modular monolithot is mérlegel.

followUps.den_microservice_boundaries_followup: Milyen tünetek mutatják, hogy két service valójában egy distributed monolith?

questions.title: The shared-database microservice trap
questions.prompt: Several services directly read and write the same SQL schema. What risks does this create, and how would you migrate away from it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Coupling risks, ownership, migration sequence, and consistency.

expectedPoints.label: Explains hidden coupling
expectedPoints.description: Covers schema coordination, bypassed business rules, lock contention, security exposure, and coupled releases.

expectedPoints.label: Establishes ownership
expectedPoints.description: Assigns each data set and invariant to one service and routes changes through that service.

expectedPoints.label: Plans incremental migration
expectedPoints.description: Uses facades, change data capture or events, expand-and-contract, and consumer-by-consumer cutover.

expectedPoints.label: Handles cross-service workflows
expectedPoints.description: Uses local transactions, outbox, sagas or compensating actions instead of accidental multi-service SQL transactions.

followUps.prompt: Which table or write path would you migrate first, and what evidence would guide that choice?

hu.title: A shared database microservice csapdája
hu.prompt: Több service közvetlenül olvassa és írja ugyanazt az SQL schemát. Milyen kockázatokat okoz ez, és hogyan migrálnál el róla?
hu.answerStructureHint: Coupling risk, ownership, migration sequence és consistency.

den_shared_db_risks.label: Elmagyarázza a rejtett couplingot
den_shared_db_risks.description: Schema coordinationt, megkerült business rule-okat, lock contentiont, security exposure-t és coupled release-t említ.

den_shared_db_owner.label: Ownershipet állapít meg
den_shared_db_owner.description: Minden data setet és invariantot egy service-hez rendel, a change-et ezen keresztül vezeti.

den_shared_db_migrate.label: Incremental migrationt tervez
den_shared_db_migrate.description: Facade-ot, CDC-t vagy eventet, expand-and-contractot és consumerenkénti cutovert használ.

den_shared_db_consistency.label: Kezeli a cross-service workflow-t
den_shared_db_consistency.description: Local transactiont, outboxot, sagát vagy compensating actiont használ accidental multi-service SQL transaction helyett.

followUps.den_shared_database_followup: Melyik table-t vagy write pathot migrálnád először, és milyen evidence alapján?

questions.title: Using Conway's Law deliberately
questions.prompt: What is Conway's Law, and how should it influence the design and ownership of an API platform?
questions.answerStructureHint: Observation, architectural consequence, and organizational action.

expectedPoints.label: Defines Conway's Law
expectedPoints.description: Explains that system structures tend to mirror the communication structures of the organizations that design them.

expectedPoints.label: Connects teams and architecture
expectedPoints.description: Shows how shared ownership and frequent cross-team coordination create coupled interfaces and releases.

expectedPoints.label: Uses the inverse Conway maneuver
expectedPoints.description: Considers aligning team ownership and communication with the desired modular architecture.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids determinism
expectedPoints.description: Treats the law as a strong influence and validates technical boundaries with domain and operational needs.

followUps.prompt: What organizational change might be needed if one feature always requires four teams to coordinate?

hu.title: Conway's Law tudatos használata
hu.prompt: Mi Conway's Law, és hogyan befolyásolja egy API platform designját és ownershipjét?
hu.answerStructureHint: Megfigyelés, architectural consequence és organizational action.

den_conway_definition.label: Definiálja Conway's Law-t
den_conway_definition.description: Elmagyarázza, hogy a system structure hajlamos tükrözni a tervező szervezet communication structure-jét.

den_conway_effect.label: Összekapcsolja a csapatot és architecture-t
den_conway_effect.description: Megmutatja, hogyan hoz létre a shared ownership és gyakori cross-team coordination coupled interface-t és release-t.

den_conway_inverse.label: Használja az inverse Conway maneuvert
den_conway_inverse.description: A team ownershipet és communicationt a kívánt modular architecture-höz igazítja.

den_conway_limits.label: Kerüli a determinizmust
den_conway_limits.description: A law-t erős befolyásként kezeli, a boundaryt domain- és operational need alapján is validálja.

followUps.den_conway_law_followup: Milyen organizational change lehet szükséges, ha egy feature mindig négy csapat koordinációját igényli?

questions.title: From object-oriented analysis to design
questions.prompt: Take a renewal-payment use case and show how you move from object-oriented analysis to object-oriented design.
questions.answerStructureHint: Use case, domain concepts, responsibilities, collaborations, and validation.

expectedPoints.label: Starts with the problem model
expectedPoints.description: Identifies actors, use cases, domain language, rules, events, and invariants before selecting classes.

expectedPoints.label: Assigns cohesive responsibilities
expectedPoints.description: Places decisions with objects or services that have the relevant information and reason to change.

expectedPoints.label: Designs collaborations and boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Explains commands or messages between objects and separates domain decisions from HTTP and persistence.

expectedPoints.label: Tests the design against scenarios
expectedPoints.description: Uses happy path, failure cases, concurrency, and future changes to evaluate the model.

followUps.prompt: Where should the rule 'a renewal cannot be paid after the final deadline' live, and why?

hu.title: Object-oriented analysistól a designig
hu.prompt: Egy renewal-payment use case példáján mutasd be, hogyan jutsz object-oriented analysisből object-oriented designhoz.
hu.answerStructureHint: Use case, domain concept, responsibility, collaboration és validáció.

den_oo_analysis.label: A problem modelből indul
den_oo_analysis.description: Actort, use case-t, domain language-et, rule-t, eventet és invariantot azonosít classválasztás előtt.

den_oo_responsibility.label: Koherens responsibilityt rendel
den_oo_responsibility.description: A döntést ahhoz az objecthez vagy service-hez teszi, amelynek megvan az információja és releváns reason to change-e.

den_oo_collaboration.label: Collaborationt és boundaryt tervez
den_oo_collaboration.description: Objectek közötti commandot vagy message-et magyaráz, és leválasztja a domain decisiont a HTTP-ről és persistence-ről.

den_oo_validate.label: Scenariókkal teszteli a designt
den_oo_validate.description: Happy path, failure case, concurrency és jövőbeli change alapján értékeli a modellt.

followUps.den_ooa_ood_followup: Hol éljen az a rule, hogy a final deadline után renewal nem fizethető, és miért?

questions.title: Applying SOLID without ceremony
questions.prompt: Give a concrete example of using SOLID principles in an API service. Focus on responsibility and dependency direction rather than reciting the acronym.
questions.answerStructureHint: Problem, principle, refactoring, and trade-off.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from a concrete change problem
expectedPoints.description: Describes code with mixed responsibilities, volatile dependencies, or difficult substitution and testing.

expectedPoints.label: Applies SRP or dependency inversion correctly
expectedPoints.description: Separates cohesive policy from infrastructure and makes high-level logic depend on an inward-owned abstraction.

expectedPoints.label: Preserves behavioral contracts
expectedPoints.description: Mentions focused interfaces and substitutability rather than merely adding interfaces.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids over-engineering
expectedPoints.description: Explains when an abstraction is unnecessary and keeps the solution proportional to expected change.

followUps.prompt: Who should own the abstraction: the high-level business layer or the database adapter? Why?

hu.title: SOLID alkalmazása ceremony nélkül

hu.answerStructureHint: Probléma, principle, refactoring és trade-off.

den_solid_problem.label: Konkrét change-problémából indul
den_solid_problem.description: Mixed responsibilityvel, volatile dependencyvel vagy nehéz substitutionnel és testinggel rendelkező kódot ír le.

den_solid_srp_dip.label: Helyesen alkalmazza az SRP-t vagy dependency inversiont
den_solid_srp_dip.description: Elválasztja a koherens policyt az infrastruktúrától, és a high-level logic inward-owned abstractiontól függ.

den_solid_tradeoff.label: Kerüli az over-engineeringet
den_solid_tradeoff.description: Elmagyarázza, mikor felesleges az abstraction, és a várható change-hez arányos marad.

questions.title: Reviewing a Liskov substitution violation

expectedPoints.label: Identifies the broken behavioral contract
expectedPoints.description: Explains that callers cannot safely use the subtype wherever the base abstraction is expected.

expectedPoints.label: Explains strengthened preconditions
expectedPoints.description: Shows that the subtype accepts fewer valid inputs than promised by the base contract.

expectedPoints.label: Proposes a clearer abstraction
expectedPoints.description: Suggests capability-specific interfaces, composition, separate strategies, or a contract that explicitly models unsupported methods.

expectedPoints.label: Uses contract tests
expectedPoints.description: Tests every implementation against the shared behavior expected by callers.

hu.title: Liskov substitution violation review

den_lsp_contract.label: Azonosítja a megsértett behavioral contractot
den_lsp_contract.description: Elmagyarázza, hogy a caller nem használhatja biztonságosan a subtype-ot ott, ahol a base abstractiont várja.

den_lsp_precondition.label: Elmagyarázza a strengthened preconditiont
den_lsp_precondition.description: Megmutatja, hogy a subtype kevesebb valid inputot fogad el, mint amit a base contract ígér.

den_lsp_redesign.label: Tisztább abstractiont javasol
den_lsp_redesign.description: Capability-specific interface-t, compositiont, külön strategyt vagy unsupported operationt explicit modellező contractot ajánl.

den_lsp_tests.label: Contract testet használ
den_lsp_tests.description: Minden implementációt a caller által elvárt közös behavior ellen tesztel.

followUps.den_liskov_review_followup: Hogyan terveznéd át az interface-eket, ha egyes processorok cardot, mások csak bank transfert támogatnak?

questions.title: Structuring a modern .NET service

expectedPoints.description: Assigns HTTP mapping, use-case coordination, business rules, and technical adapters to clear responsibilities.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps dependencies pointing inward

expectedPoints.label: Defines use-case and transaction boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Explains where validation, authorization context, business invariants, and persistence commit occur.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids empty layers
expectedPoints.description: Keeps the number of projects and abstractions proportional to domain complexity and change risk.

hu.title: Modern .NET service strukturálása

den_dotnet_transaction.label: Use-case és transaction boundaryt definiál
den_dotnet_transaction.description: Elmagyarázza, hol történik validation, authorization context kezelése, business invariant és persistence commit.

den_dotnet_pragmatic.label: Kerüli az üres layereket
den_dotnet_pragmatic.description: A projectek és abstractionök számát a domain complexityhez és change riskhez igazítja.

followUps.den_dotnet_architecture_followup: Hova tennél egy payment notification küldésére szolgáló interface-t, és hol élne az implementációja?

questions.title: ASP.NET Core middleware order
questions.prompt: Explain how the ASP.NET Core request pipeline works. Why does middleware order matter?
questions.answerStructureHint: Chain behavior, short-circuiting, ordering examples, and boundaries.

expectedPoints.label: Explains the middleware chain
expectedPoints.description: Describes before-and-after processing around a next delegate and possible short-circuiting.

expectedPoints.description: Uses examples involving routing, authentication, authorization, exception handling, CORS, or static files.

den_pipeline_limits.label: A domain logicot middleware-en kívül tartja
den_pipeline_limits.description: Felismeri, hogy a middleware nem business use-case rule-ok helye.

followUps.den_aspnet_pipeline_followup: Mi romolhat el, ha az authorization az authentication előtt fut?

questions.title: Choosing .NET DI lifetimes

expectedPoints.label: Defines all three lifetimes

hu.title: .NET DI lifetime választás

den_di_state.label: Figyelembe veszi a state-et és thread-safetyt
den_di_state.description: Singletonhoz immutable vagy szinkronizált shared state-et választ, és kerüli a rejtett request datát.

den_di_disposal.label: Számol a resource ownershippel
den_di_disposal.description: Említi a container általi disposalt, és kerüli a manual ownership keverését.

questions.title: Async and await on the server

expectedPoints.label: Explains non-blocking I/O waiting
expectedPoints.description: States that await frees the current thread while asynchronous I/O is pending and resumes through a continuation.

expectedPoints.label: Separates async from parallel CPU work
expectedPoints.description: Explains that async does not inherently make CPU-bound work faster or make shared state thread-safe.

expectedPoints.label: Names server-side mistakes
expectedPoints.description: Mentions .Result or .Wait, unnecessary Task.Run, sync-over-async, fire-and-forget, or unbounded Task.WhenAll.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps the call chain asynchronous

den_async_not_parallel.label: Elválasztja az asyncot a parallel CPU-munkától
den_async_not_parallel.description: Elmagyarázza, hogy az async önmagában nem gyorsítja a CPU-bound munkát és nem teszi thread-safe-fé a shared state-et.

den_async_mistakes.label: Szerveroldali hibákat nevez meg

questions.title: Cancellation and bounded concurrency

expectedPoints.label: Defines failure and partial-result semantics

den_cancel_propagate.label: Kooperatívan propagálja a cancellationt

den_cancel_timeout.label: Explicit time budgetet használ

den_cancel_bound.label: Korlátozza a concurrencyt

den_cancel_result.label: Failure- és partial-result semanticset definiál

questions.title: Diagnosing an EF Core performance problem

expectedPoints.label: Finds N+1 and loading issues

den_ef_nplusone.label: Megtalálja az N+1 és loading problémát

den_ef_index.description: Filtert, sortingot, cardinalityt, indexet, statisticset és query selectivityt ellenőriz.

followUps.den_ef_query_followup: Hogyan válhat az eager loading is performance-problémává?

expectedPoints.label: Uses optimistic concurrency where appropriate

expectedPoints.label: Defines conflict behavior

expectedPoints.label: Uses expand-and-contract

expectedPoints.label: Plans operational safety

hu.answerStructureHint: Concurrency token, conflict policy, expand-and-contract és rollout.

den_ef_concurrency.label: Optimistic concurrencyt használ, ahol megfelelő

den_ef_conflict.label: Conflict behaviort definiál

den_ef_expand.label: Expand-and-contractot használ

den_ef_ops.label: Operational safetyt tervez

followUps.den_ef_concurrency_migration_followup: Miért veszélyes rolling deployment közben ugyanabban a release-ben átnevezni a production columnt és módosítani a kódot?

questions.title: Designing a relational model

expectedPoints.label: Chooses identifiers and uniqueness
expectedPoints.description: Uses primary keys plus business unique constraints such as one renewal per application and year.

expectedPoints.label: Uses database integrity constraints
expectedPoints.description: Applies foreign keys, nullability, checks, and atomic constraints for rules that must survive concurrent writers.

expectedPoints.label: Connects the model to access patterns

followUps.prompt: Why is a generated primary key not enough to prevent two renewals for the same application and year?

hu.answerStructureHint: Entityk, relationship-ek, key-ek, constraint-ek és access patternök.

den_rel_entities.label: Entityket és relationship-eket azonosít
den_rel_entities.description: Elválasztja a stabil business concept-eket, és explicit módon modellezi a one-to-many vagy optional kapcsolatokat.

den_rel_keys.label: Identifiert és uniqueness-t választ
den_rel_keys.description: Primary key mellett business unique constraintet használ, például application és year páronként egy renewal.

den_rel_integrity.label: Database integrity constraint-eket használ

den_rel_access.description: Gyakori queryt, indexet, data volume-ot, historyt és audit requirementet mérlegel.

followUps.den_relational_modeling_followup: Miért nem elég egy generated primary key ahhoz, hogy megakadályozza az ugyanahhoz az applicationhöz és évhez tartozó két renewalt?

questions.title: Diagnosing a slow SQL query

expectedPoints.label: Designs the index for the access pattern
expectedPoints.description: Considers equality filters, range or sort columns, selectivity, key order, and included columns.

expectedPoints.label: Accounts for index cost
expectedPoints.description: Mentions write overhead, storage, maintenance, duplicate indexes, and changing workloads.

expectedPoints.label: Verifies instead of assuming

followUps.prompt: How would you choose the order of customer, status, and deadline in a composite index?

hu.title: Lassú SQL query diagnosztizálása

den_sql_plan.label: Execution planből és mérésből indul
den_sql_plan.description: Actual plant, row-kat, scaneket, estimate-et, waitet, durationt és representative production paramétert ellenőriz.

den_sql_verify.label: Feltételezés helyett verifikál
den_sql_verify.description: Újrafuttatja a plant és load testet, production metricet figyel és más queryk regressionjét ellenőrzi.

followUps.den_sql_index_followup: Hogyan választanád meg a customer, status és deadline sorrendjét egy composite indexben?

questions.title: Transactions and isolation

expectedPoints.label: Explains the useful ACID guarantees

expectedPoints.label: Understands concurrency anomalies
expectedPoints.description: Mentions dirty, non-repeatable, phantom, lost update, or write-skew risks where relevant.

expectedPoints.label: Starts from the business invariant
expectedPoints.description: Defines what must not happen, such as charging twice or paying a closed renewal, before choosing a level.

expectedPoints.label: Balances isolation and throughput

expectedPoints.label: Explains NoSQL strengths and limits

expectedPoints.label: Includes operational and team factors

questions.title: Resilient partner integration
questions.prompt: A partner API is slow, intermittently fails, and sometimes completes a request after your timeout. How would you design the integration?
questions.answerStructureHint: Timeouts, safe retry, isolation, state tracking, and recovery.

expectedPoints.label: Uses explicit timeouts and budgets
expectedPoints.description: Sets connect and operation timeouts within an overall request or job deadline.

expectedPoints.label: Makes retries safe
expectedPoints.description: Uses idempotency keys or operation identifiers and distinguishes retryable from permanent failures.

expectedPoints.label: Controls retry pressure
expectedPoints.description: Uses limited attempts, exponential backoff, jitter, circuit breaking, and bounded concurrency.

expectedPoints.label: Handles unknown outcomes
expectedPoints.description: Stores operation state, queries status where possible, reconciles later, and supports manual recovery.

expectedPoints.label: Adds operational visibility
expectedPoints.description: Tracks dependency latency, error categories, retry counts, circuit state, and SLO impact.

followUps.prompt: What should your system do when the timeout means you do not know whether the partner processed the request?

hu.title: Resilient partner integration
hu.prompt: Egy partner API lassú, időnként hibázik, és néha a timeoutod után mégis befejezi a requestet. Hogyan terveznéd az integrationt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Timeout, safe retry, isolation, state tracking és recovery.

den_res_timeout.label: Explicit timeoutot és budgetet használ
den_res_timeout.description: Connect- és operation timeoutot állít overall request- vagy job deadline-on belül.

den_res_idempotent.label: Biztonságossá teszi a retryt
den_res_idempotent.description: Idempotency keyt vagy operation identifiert használ, és elválasztja a retryable és permanent failure-t.

den_res_backoff.label: Kontrollálja a retry pressure-t
den_res_backoff.description: Limited attemptet, exponential backoffot, jittert, circuit breakert és bounded concurrencyt használ.

den_res_reconcile.label: Kezeli az unknown outcome-ot
den_res_reconcile.description: Operation state-et tárol, ahol lehet statuszt kérdez, később reconcile-ol és manual recoveryt támogat.

den_res_observe.label: Operational visibilityt ad
den_res_observe.description: Dependency latencyt, error categoryt, retry countot, circuit state-et és SLO impactot követ.

followUps.den_resilience_integration_followup: Mit tegyen a rendszer, amikor timeout miatt nem tudod, hogy a partner feldolgozta-e a requestet?

questions.title: TDD and an architecture-level test strategy
questions.prompt: What is TDD, and how would you create a test strategy for a .NET microservice platform without relying mainly on end-to-end tests?
questions.answerStructureHint: Red-green-refactor, test boundaries, contracts, and confidence.

expectedPoints.label: Explains red-green-refactor
expectedPoints.description: Writes a failing behavior test, implements the smallest change, and improves the design while keeping tests green.

expectedPoints.label: Matches test type to risk
expectedPoints.description: Uses unit tests for domain rules, integration tests for infrastructure, contract tests for service interfaces, and few E2E flows.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids meaningless mocks
expectedPoints.description: Uses real database or broker integration where their behavior matters and keeps mocks at controlled external boundaries.

expectedPoints.label: Covers quality beyond test count
expectedPoints.description: Includes deterministic tests, maintainability, failure diagnosis, CI speed, production monitoring, and safe rollout.

followUps.prompt: Which test would catch an incompatible response field change between two independently deployed services?

hu.title: TDD és architecture-level test strategy
hu.prompt: Mi a TDD, és hogyan készítenél test strategyt .NET microservice platformhoz anélkül, hogy főként end-to-end testekre támaszkodnál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Red-green-refactor, test boundary, contract és confidence.

den_tdd_cycle.label: Elmagyarázza a red-green-refactor ciklust
den_tdd_cycle.description: Failing behavior testet ír, a legkisebb change-et implementálja, majd green test mellett javítja a designt.

den_test_layers.label: A test type-ot riskhez illeszti
den_test_layers.description: Domain rule-ra unit testet, infrastruktúrára integration testet, service interface-re contract testet, és kevés E2E flow-t használ.

den_test_realism.label: Kerüli az értelmetlen mockot
den_test_realism.description: Valós database- vagy broker integrationt használ, ahol behavioruk számít, mockot pedig kontrollált external boundaryn.

den_test_quality.label: Test counton túl is kezeli a qualityt
den_test_quality.description: Determinismust, maintainabilityt, failure diagnosist, CI speedet, production monitoringot és safe rolloutot foglal bele.

followUps.den_tdd_testing_followup: Melyik test kapná el két independently deployed service között az inkompatibilis response-field változást?

questions.title: Resolving a technical disagreement
questions.prompt: Two senior developers strongly disagree about introducing a message broker. As the architect or technical lead, how would you reach and communicate a decision?
questions.answerStructureHint: Decision framing, evidence, participation, ownership, and follow-through.

expectedPoints.label: Frames the decision with requirements
expectedPoints.description: Clarifies the problem, NFRs, constraints, decision deadline, and what success means before debating technology.

expectedPoints.label: Compares options with evidence
expectedPoints.description: Documents alternatives, trade-offs, risks, operational cost, and uses a spike or data where uncertainty is material.

expectedPoints.label: Creates a fair decision process
expectedPoints.description: Invites relevant builders and operators, names a decision owner, prevents endless consensus seeking, and records dissent.

expectedPoints.label: Communicates and revisits the decision
expectedPoints.description: Writes the rationale, consequences, rollout, review date, and signals that would trigger reconsideration.

followUps.prompt: What do you do if consensus is impossible but the delivery deadline is approaching?

hu.title: Technikai vita feloldása
hu.prompt: Két senior developer erősen vitázik egy message broker bevezetéséről. Architectként vagy technical leadként hogyan jutnál döntésre, és hogyan kommunikálnád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Decision framing, evidence, részvétel, ownership és follow-through.

den_lead_frame.label: Requirement alapján keretezi a döntést
den_lead_frame.description: A technológiavita előtt tisztázza a problémát, NFR-eket, constraint-eket, deadline-t és success jelentését.

den_lead_options.label: Evidence alapján hasonlítja össze az opciókat
den_lead_options.description: Alternatívát, trade-offot, risket és operational costot dokumentál, és material uncertainty esetén spike-ot vagy adatot használ.

den_lead_process.label: Fair decision processt teremt
den_lead_process.description: Bevonja a releváns buildert és operatort, decision ownert nevez meg, megelőzi a végtelen consensus-keresést és rögzíti a dissentet.

den_lead_communicate.label: Kommunikálja és újravizsgálja a döntést
den_lead_communicate.description: Leírja a rationale-t, consequence-et, rolloutot, review date-et és reconsideration triggert.

followUps.den_technical_disagreement_followup: Mit teszel, ha consensus nem érhető el, de közeledik a delivery deadline?

questions.title: Coaching a team while raising quality
questions.prompt: How would you improve architecture and code quality across a team without becoming the reviewer and decision bottleneck?
questions.answerStructureHint: Shared standards, automation, delegation, feedback, and outcomes.

expectedPoints.label: Creates risk-based shared standards
expectedPoints.description: Defines a small set of examples, design principles, review checklists, and quality gates tied to real failure modes.

expectedPoints.label: Automates repeatable checks
expectedPoints.description: Uses formatting, static analysis, tests, security scans, templates, and CI policies so humans focus on judgment.

expectedPoints.label: Builds distributed ownership
expectedPoints.description: Uses pairing, rotating reviewers, design-review facilitation, and progressively delegates decisions.

expectedPoints.label: Measures outcomes and adapts
expectedPoints.description: Tracks defects, lead time, incidents, review quality, and team feedback rather than counting rules or meetings.

followUps.prompt: How do you decide which architecture decisions can be delegated and which need wider review?

hu.title: Csapat coaching és quality emelése
hu.prompt: Hogyan javítanád a csapat architecture- és code qualityjét anélkül, hogy te lennél minden review és döntés bottleneckje?
hu.answerStructureHint: Shared standard, automation, delegation, feedback és outcome.

den_coach_standards.label: Risk-based shared standardot hoz létre
den_coach_standards.description: Kevés példát, design principle-t, review checklistet és valós failure mode-hoz kötött quality gate-et definiál.

den_coach_automation.label: Automatizálja az ismételhető checkeket
den_coach_automation.description: Formattinget, static analysist, testet, security scant, templatet és CI policyt használ, hogy az ember judgmentre fókuszáljon.

den_coach_delegate.label: Distributed ownershipet épít
den_coach_delegate.description: Pairinget, rotating reviewert, design-review facilitációt használ, és fokozatosan delegál döntéseket.

den_coach_measure.label: Outcome-ot mér és alkalmazkodik
den_coach_measure.description: Defectet, lead time-ot, incidentet, review qualityt és team feedbacket követ szabály- vagy meeting-szám helyett.

followUps.den_coaching_quality_followup: Hogyan döntöd el, mely architecture decision delegálható, és melyik igényel szélesebb review-t?

questions.title: Architecture in an agile Scrum environment
questions.prompt: How do you perform architecture work in Scrum without either doing big design up front or letting every sprint create accidental architecture?
questions.answerStructureHint: Just-enough design, risk spikes, runway, feedback, and ownership.

expectedPoints.label: Uses just-enough upfront design
expectedPoints.description: Clarifies high-risk decisions, boundaries, NFRs, and irreversible choices while leaving low-risk details to implementation.

expectedPoints.label: Evolves architecture incrementally
expectedPoints.description: Slices work into vertical increments, preserves compatibility, and uses refactoring and migration steps.

expectedPoints.label: Uses spikes for uncertainty
expectedPoints.description: Time-boxes experiments and records decisions instead of letting research become open-ended.

expectedPoints.label: Builds feedback into delivery
expectedPoints.description: Uses review, tests, telemetry, retrospectives, and architecture checkpoints based on risk rather than ceremonial approval.

followUps.prompt: Which architecture decisions would you make before the first sprint, and which would you deliberately postpone?

hu.title: Architecture agilis Scrum környezetben
hu.prompt: Hogyan végzel architecture munkát Scrumban úgy, hogy ne legyen big design up front, de minden sprint se hozzon létre accidental architecture-t?
hu.answerStructureHint: Just-enough design, risk spike, runway, feedback és ownership.

den_scrum_enough.label: Just-enough upfront designt használ
den_scrum_enough.description: Tisztázza a high-risk decisiont, boundaryt, NFR-t és irreversible choice-ot, az alacsony kockázatú részleteket implementációra hagyja.

den_scrum_incremental.label: Incrementálisan fejleszti az architecture-t
den_scrum_incremental.description: Vertical incrementre bont, compatibilityt őriz, és refactoringot valamint migration stepet használ.

den_scrum_spikes.label: Spike-ot használ uncertaintyre
den_scrum_spikes.description: Time-boxolja a kísérletet és döntést rögzít, nem hagyja open-endeddé válni a kutatást.

den_scrum_feedback.label: Feedbacket épít a deliverybe
den_scrum_feedback.description: Review-t, testet, telemetryt, retrospective-et és risk-based architecture checkpointot használ ceremonial approval helyett.

followUps.den_scrum_architecture_followup: Mely architecture decisionöket hoznád meg az első sprint előtt, és melyeket halasztanál tudatosan?
